cells.label: isolating
cells.function: no inflectional morphology — words are monosyllabic; grammar is by word order + particles

conditioning: none — no affixation; grammatical relations carried by word order, classifiers, and free particles

fit_none.note: the CORRECT answer for vie is the absence of inflectional paradigms; inventing case/gender/voice here would be the failure mode

evidence_citations.stat: ~1.03 syllables/word, ~97% monosyllabic over the frequent vocabulary
the monosyllabic signal holds despite the inducer's (spurious) affixes — no real affixation

prose: vie is morphologically ISOLATING: words are overwhelmingly monosyllabic (~1.03 syllables/word, 97% monosyllabic) and carry no inflectional affixation. Grammar is expressed by word order, classifiers, and free grammatical particles, not by case/gender/tense morphology. The correct finding is the ABSENCE of inflectional paradigms — the system should report that, not invent one. (The syllable signal is robust to the inducer's spurious over-segmentation, which had wrongly made vie look fusional.) Recommendation: CONFIRM isolating; do not open case/gender/voice paradigms for vie.
